info.title: Netlify's API documentation
info.description:
Netlify is a hosting service for the programmable web. It understands your documents and provides an API to handle atomic deploys of websites, manage form submissions, inject JavaScript snippets, and much more. This is a REST-style API that uses JSON for serialization and OAuth 2 for authentication.

This document is an OpenAPI reference for the Netlify API that you can explore. For more detailed instructions for common uses, please visit the [online documentation](https://www.netlify.com/docs/api/). Visit our Community forum to join the conversation about [understanding and using Netlify's API](https://community.netlify.com/t/common-issue-understanding-and-using-netlifys-api/160).

Additionally, we have two API clients for your convenience:
- [Go Client](https://github.com/netlify/open-api#go-client)
- [JS Client](https://github.com/netlify/build/tree/main/packages/js-client)

get.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [getEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/getEnvVars) to retrieve site environment variables.

post.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [createEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/createEnvVars) to create environment variables for a site.

get.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [getEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/getEnvVars) to retrieve site environment variables.

patch.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [updateEnvVar](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/updateEnvVar) to update a site's environment variables.

post.description:
Provisions or updates a TLS certificate for the site.

**Creating a certificate (site has no certificate):**
- Omit certificate params to initiate Let's Encrypt provisioning
- Provide certificate, key, and ca_certificates to upload a custom certificate

**Updating a certificate (site already has a certificate):**
- REQUIRES certificate, key, and ca_certificates to replace with a new custom certificate
- Use POST /api/v1/sites/{site_id}/ssl/renew to renew an existing Let's Encrypt certificate

parameters.name: certificate
parameters.description: PEM-encoded certificate. Required when updating an existing certificate.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: PEM-encoded private key. Required when updating an existing certificate.

parameters.name: ca_certificates
parameters.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate chain. Required when updating an existing certificate.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. Returns errors such as: "certificate parameter is required when updating an existing certificate" (when updating without params), "No custom domain configured", or "bad dns for custom domain"

get.description: Returns all environment variables for an account or site. An account corresponds to a team in the Netlify UI.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If specified, only return environment variables set on this site

post.description: Creates new environment variables. Granular scopes are available on Pro plans and above.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, create an environment variable on the site level, not the account level

key.description: The existing or new name of the key, if you wish to rename it (case-sensitive)

scopes.description: The scopes that this environment variable is set to (Pro plans and above)

is_secret.description: Secret values are only readable by code running on Netlify's systems. With secrets, only the local development context values are readable from the UI, API, and CLI. By default, environment variable values are not secret.

get.description: Returns all environment variables for a site. This convenience method behaves the same as `getEnvVars` but doesn't require an `account_id` as input.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, return the environment variable for a specific site (no merging is performed)

put.description: Updates an existing environment variable and all of its values. Existing values will be replaced by values provided.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The existing environment variable key name (case-sensitive)

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, update an environment variable set on this site

key.description: The existing or new name of the key, if you wish to rename it (case-sensitive)

scopes.description: The scopes that this environment variable is set to (Pro plans and above)

is_secret.description: Secret values are only readable by code running on Netlify's systems. With secrets, only the local development context values are readable from the UI, API, and CLI. By default, environment variable values are not secret.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, delete the environment variable from this site

patch.description: Updates or creates a new value for an existing environment variable.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The existing environment variable key name (case-sensitive)

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, update an environment variable set on this site

context.description: The deploy context in which this value will be used. `dev` refers to local development when running `netlify dev`. `branch` must be provided with a value in `context_parameter`.

context_parameter.description: An additional parameter for custom branches. Currently, this is used for providing a branch name when `context=branch`.

delete.description: Deletes a specific environment variable value.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: If provided, delete the value from an environment variable on this site

post.description: Purges cached content from Netlify's CDN. Supports purging by Cache-Tag.

Runs a build for a site. The build will be scheduled to run at the first opportunity, but it might not start immediately if insufficient account build capacity is available.

Files for build can be uploaded as a zipped site using one of these methods:
1. Set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip file as the raw request body
2. Set Content-Type to 'multipart/form-data' and include the zip file in the 'zip' field

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: If no branch is specified, it is treated as a production deploy If a branch IS specified and matches the main branch, it is also production If a branch is specified and doesn't match the main branch, it is a branch deploy

parameters.name: clear_cache
parameters.description: Whether to clear the build cache before building

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The build image tag to use for the build

parameters.name: template_id
parameters.description: The build template to use for the build

zip.description:
A zip file containing the site files to build.
Only used with Content-Type 'multipart/form-data'.
Alternatively, set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip as the raw request body (no 'zip' parameter needed).

put.description:
[Beta] Unlinks the repo from the site.

This action will also:
- Delete associated deploy keys
- Delete outgoing webhooks for the repo
- Delete the site's build hooks
Re-enables a site that was previously disabled by the user. Sites that were disabled for usage exceeded or marked as spam cannot be re-enabled via this endpoint.
Disables a site, preventing it from serving content. The site can be re-enabled later using the enable endpoint.

patch.description: Updates the deploy validations report for a deploy.

get.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [getEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/getEnvVars) to retrieve site environment variables.

post.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [createEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/createEnvVars) to create environment variables for a site.

200.description: get all DNS zones the user has access to

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: the account of the dns zone

parameters.name: transfer_account_id
parameters.description: the account you want to transfer the dns zone to

parameters.name: transfer_user_id
parameters.description: the user you want to transfer the dns zone to

200.description: transfer a DNS zone to another account
get all DNS records for a single DNS zone
The mapping of providers and supported models

get.description: Returns an AI Gateway token for a specific site

200.description: AI Gateway token for the site

404.description: AI Gateway not available for this site

get.description: Returns an AI Gateway token scoped to an account

200.description: AI Gateway token for the account

404.description: AI Gateway not available for this account

get.description: Returns the database connection string for the specified site.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: The database role to use for the connection string. Defaults to netlifydb_owner if not specified.

post.description: Creates a new database for the specified site. If a database already exists, returns the existing connection string. The database region defaults to the site's functions region if not specified.

delete.description: Deletes the database and all associated branches and snapshots for the specified site.

post.description: Creates a new database branch. If a branch already exists for the specified branch ID, returns the existing connection string.

get.description: Returns all branches for the site's database with compute status and metadata.
Returns the database branch connection string for a specific branch.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: The database role to use for the connection string. Defaults to netlifydb_owner if not specified.

put.description: Sets compute settings for a specific database branch, overriding project-level settings. Requires a Pro or higher plan.

403.description: Compute customization requires a Pro or higher plan

get.description: Returns the project-level compute settings for the database. Returns effective settings (custom or tier defaults). Requires a Pro or higher plan.

403.description: Compute customization requires a Pro or higher plan

put.description: Sets project-level compute settings for the database. Applied to new branches. Can be overridden per-branch. Requires a Pro or higher plan.

403.description: Compute customization requires a Pro or higher plan

delete.description: Resets project-level compute settings to tier defaults. Requires a Pro or higher plan.

403.description: Compute customization requires a Pro or higher plan

post.description: Runs database migrations for the specified deploy. Finds the deploy and determines the appropriate branch.

parameters.name: deploy_id
parameters.description: The deploy ID to run migrations for

409.description: Migration conflict - migration modified or removed after being applied

post.description: Creates a point-in-time snapshot of a database branch. Defaults to the production branch if no branch name is specified.

get.description: Returns all snapshots for the site's database.

post.description: Restores a snapshot to a database branch. Defaults to the production branch if no branch_name is specified.

region.description: The region where the database should be created. Defaults to the site's functions region if not specified.

createDatabaseRequest.description: Request body for creating a database

connection_string.description: The connection string for the database

databaseResponse.description: Response containing the database connection string

parent_branch_id.description: The ID of the parent branch to create the new branch from. Defaults to the production branch if not specified.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata to associate with the branch

createDatabaseBranchRequest.description: Request body for creating a database branch

connection_string.description: The connection string for the database branch

databaseBranchResponse.description: Response containing the database branch connection string

databaseBranchesResponse.description: Response containing a list of database branches

connection_string.description: The connection string for the branch

state.description: The current state of the branch

logical_size_bytes.description: The logical size of the branch in bytes

updated_at.description: When the branch was last updated

last_active_at.description: When the branch was last active

databaseBranchDetail.description: Detailed information about a database branch

current_state.description: The current state of the compute endpoint

suspend_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds of inactivity before the compute endpoint is suspended

last_active.description: When the compute endpoint was last active

databaseBranchCompute.description: Compute endpoint status for a branch

branch_id.description: The ID of the branch to snapshot. Defaults to "production" if not specified.

createDatabaseSnapshotRequest.description: Request body for creating a database snapshot

id.description: The unique identifier of the snapshot

source_branch_id.description: The ID of the branch that was snapshotted

manual.description: Whether this snapshot was manually created

timestamp.description: The point-in-time timestamp of the snapshot

databaseSnapshot.description: A point-in-time snapshot of a database branch

deploy.description: Deploy information associated with the snapshot

source.description: The source that created the snapshot

databaseSnapshotsResponse.description: Response containing a list of database snapshots

branch_id.description: The ID of the branch to restore the snapshot to. Defaults to "production" if not specified.

restoreDatabaseSnapshotRequest.description: Request body for restoring a database snapshot

min_cu.description: Minimum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be less than or equal to max_cu.

max_cu.description: Maximum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be greater than or equal to min_cu. max_cu - min_cu must not exceed 8.0.

sleep_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds of inactivity before the compute endpoint is suspended. Use -1 for always on, or a non-negative value.

databaseComputeSettingsRequest.description: Request body for setting compute settings. All fields are optional; only provided fields are updated.

databaseComputeSettings.description: Compute settings for a database or branch

dry_run.description: If true, validates migrations without applying them.

runDatabaseMigrationsRequest.description: Request body for running database migrations

id.description: The id of the deploy validations report

secretsScanMatches.description: The list of secrets scan matches

functions_dir.description: The directory where Netlify can find your compiled functions to deploy them. Defaults to netlify/functions if not set. You can also define and override this setting in your project’s netlify.toml file.

cmd.description: The build command to run. This is the command that Netlify runs to build your site. If a site has a netlify.toml file with a build command it will override this value.

stop_builds.description: When true, Netlify will not build your project automatically. You can build locally via the CLI and then publish new deploys manually via the CLI or the API.

key.description: The environment variable key, like ALGOLIA_ID (case-sensitive)

scopes.description: The scopes that this environment variable is set to

values.description: An array of Value objects containing values and metadata

is_secret.description: Secret values are only readable by code running on Netlify's systems. With secrets, only the local development context values are readable from the UI, API, and CLI. By default, environment variable values are not secret.

updated_at.description: The timestamp of when the value was last updated

id.description: The environment variable value's universally unique ID

context.description: The deploy context in which this value will be used. `dev` refers to local development when running `netlify dev`.

context_parameter.description: An additional parameter for custom branches. Currently, this is used for specifying a branch name when `context=branch`.

full_name.description: The user's full name (first and last)

avatar_url.description: A URL pointing to the user's avatar

files.description: A hash mapping file paths to SHA1 digests of the file contents.

zip.description:
A zip file containing the site files to deploy. Alternative to 'files'.
To use this field, set Content-Type to 'application/json' and include the zip content here.
Alternatively, you can set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip as the raw request body (not as JSON).

environment.description:
A list of deploy-specific environment variable data. Data specified this way applies only
to this specific deploy and is merged into any existing environment variables set on the
account and site.

Deploy-specific environment variable data takes precedence over account and site
environment variable data: For example, a deploy-specific variable with the key `NODE_ENV`
will take priority over any existing site- and account-level environment variable data
with the key `NODE_ENV`.

Environment variable data may be provided at one of two times:

- When creating a new Deploy with deploy files (most common)
- When finalizing an existing Deploy with deploy files

Once set, environment variables for a specific deploy cannot be modified. Subsequent
attempts to modify environment variable data for a deploy will be ignored.

deployFiles.description:
Deploy files can be provided in two ways:
1. As a JSON object using 'files' (a hash mapping file paths to SHA1 digests), OR
2. As a zip file using one of these methods:
   - Set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip file as the raw request body
   - Include the zip file content in the 'zip' field of this JSON object with Content-Type 'application/json'

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp when the token expires
